questions.title: Edit surface: single-artifact swap or multi-artifact patch set?
questions.context: Run bundle layout (Phase 9 Q-07): runs/<id>/{manifest, intent, plan, execution-plan, piles/<kind>/iter-<N>/, execution/{journal,snapshot}, review-gate, evaluation-report, evolution/snapshot, ci-events}. The phase goal mentions "edit a single recorded artifact" but multi-edit is plausible for compound counterfactuals (e.g. judge X + reviewer Y).

options.label: Single-artifact swap only (v1)
options.description: Each replay edits exactly one recorded artifact. Simpler determinism contract; clearer post-mortem story ("would judge X have unblocked this?"). Multi-edit deferred to a later phase.

options.label: Multi-artifact patch set (declarative)
options.description: Operator supplies an EditSet manifest mapping {artifactPath → newContent}. More expressive; lets you simulate combined fixes. Bigger schema/CLI surface; harder to argue determinism without ordering rules.

options.label: Single in v1, design schema for multi
options.description: CLI accepts only one --edit in v1, but the EditSet on-disk schema supports N edits so v2 unlocks multi without a breaking change.

questions.title: Which artifact kinds are editable in v1?
questions.context: Phase goal lists at minimum: judge verdict, reviewer comment, task result, raw LLM completion. Bundle has many more (manifest, plan, execution-plan, intent, admission decisions, evolution snapshots). Editing admission/intent would invalidate prior gates.

options.label: Pile outputs only
options.description: Only piles/<kind>/iter-<N>/{result.json,trace.json} (judge, reviewer, planning, etc.) — matches the phase’s stated minimum and keeps replay below the admission boundary.

options.label: Pile outputs + execution journal entries
options.description: Adds task results from execution/journal.jsonl. Lets you A/B "what if this command had succeeded?" but mutating an append-only journal needs a careful overlay model.

options.label: Pile + journal + raw LLM completions
options.description: Also exposes per-call LLM completion records (if recorded as separate artifacts). Maximum counterfactual power; biggest surface to schema-pin.

options.label: Allowlist pinned in SEED — explicit kinds, nothing else
options.description: Discuss-phase enumerates the exact kinds (e.g. {pile.result, pile.trace, journal.taskResult, llmCompletion}); anything not on the list is refused at the CLI.

questions.title: How are editable artifacts addressed?
questions.context: Run bundle paths are relative to runs/<id>/. Pile artifacts have stable paths (piles/<kind>/iter-<N>/result.json). LLM completions and journal entries are inside aggregate files.

options.label: Relative path (e.g. piles/review/iter-2/result.json)
options.description: Simple, matches the inspect command’s path-indexed view. Doesn’t address sub-records inside aggregate files.

options.label: Path + JSON Pointer for sub-records
options.description: e.g. execution/journal.jsonl#/lines/42 or piles/review/iter-2/result.json#/findings/3. More expressive; needs an addressing schema.

options.label: Content-hash address (sha256 of original)
options.description: Edit references the original artifact’s sha256; CLI resolves to path. Tamper-evident; awkward UX (operators must look up hashes).

options.label: Path-only in v1, JSON Pointer reserved for v2
options.description: v1 only edits whole artifacts identified by relative path; sub-record edits deferred.

questions.title: Does the edited artifact have to validate against its schema?
questions.context: Most run-bundle artifacts have JSON schemas (intent, plan, pile results, evaluation report). An invalid edit could crash downstream replay in confusing ways.

options.label: Yes — validate at replay-CLI entry, refuse on schema fail
options.description: CLI runs the same schema validation as a fresh artifact before starting replay. Refusal artifact emitted (matches admission-style UX). Strongest correctness guarantee.

options.label: Validate, but allow --force to bypass
options.description: Default validates; --force-invalid skips for niche debugging ("what happens if the judge returns malformed JSON?"). Power-user escape hatch.

options.label: No — pass through and let downstream stages refuse naturally
options.description: Replay surfaces whatever error a fresh run would surface for malformed input. Less work; harder to tell schema bugs from logic bugs in the post-mortem.

questions.title: Downstream LLM calls: replay recorded outputs, or re-call live?
questions.context: Phase 13 success criterion: "identical edited prefix → identical downstream artifacts". Determinism requires either replaying recorded LLM outputs, or treating the edit as invalidating only the artifact + its direct consumers.

options.label: Replay all downstream artifacts unchanged (cache)
options.description: Every artifact after the edit point is re-served from the bundle as-is. The edit only changes how the *consumers* of the edited artifact behave — but they too replay their recorded output unless they directly consumed the edited artifact. Matches "counterfactual without re-running upstream stages" wording.

options.label: Re-run any stage that read the edited artifact; replay everything else
options.description: Compute a dependency closure: stages that read artifact A (transitively) are re-executed (real LLM calls, real subprocesses); others are served from cache. Most realistic counterfactual; needs a recorded read-set.

options.label: Re-run everything downstream (full re-execution from edit point)
options.description: Edit point onward is a fresh run. Cheapest determinism story (no read-set tracking) but costs LLM tokens and isn’t what the phase goal describes.

questions.title: How is the determinism contract proved?
questions.context: Success criterion mandates content-hash comparison on a fixture: identical edited prefix → identical downstream artifacts.

options.label: Per-artifact sha256, asserted in the contract test
options.description: Replay produces an overlay manifest of {path → sha256}; contract test asserts byte-equality to a recorded fixture. Same model used by inspect command.

options.label: Whole-bundle merkle root
options.description: Compute one root hash over the canonicalized artifact tree. One number to compare; harder to localize a regression.

options.label: Per-stage hash chain (each stage hashes its inputs + outputs)
options.description: Stronger property: any drift surfaces at the stage that introduced it. Bigger contract surface.

questions.title: Edit-cascade scope: how do we know which artifacts a stage "read"?
questions.context: Without a recorded read-set, option Q-05(b) is infeasible. Today’s bundle records outputs; reads are implicit in code.

options.label: Static dependency map, hand-maintained in @protostar/replay
options.description: A table {stageKind → reads[]} encodes which artifacts each stage consumes. Cheap; goes stale silently.

options.label: Record a read-set during the original run (Phase 4/5 instrumentation)
options.description: Each stage emits a read-set to its trace.json: {pathsRead: [...]}. Most accurate; requires touching the execution engine and review loop.

options.label: Coarse-grained: edit invalidates the *current iter* only; later iters re-run from journal
options.description: Skip read-set tracking entirely. Anything in iter-N or later re-executes; iter-0..N-1 replay unchanged. Simple but blunt.

questions.title: Where do replayed runs live on disk?
questions.context: Original bundle must never be mutated (success criterion). Two natural shapes: a sibling replay bundle, or an overlay manifest that references the original by hash.

options.label: Sibling bundle: .protostar/runs/<runId>/replays/<replayId>/
options.description: Full bundle copy under a replays/ subdir. Self-contained; addressable by inspect; biggest disk footprint.

options.label: Sibling bundle at top level: .protostar/runs/<replayId>/ with parentRunId field
options.description: Replay is just another run, with manifest.parentRunId + manifest.editSet pointing back. Operator surface (status/inspect/prune) treats it uniformly. Storage cost: same as (a) unless deduplicated.

options.label: Overlay manifest: .protostar/runs/<runId>/replays/<replayId>/overlay.json
options.description: Only stores edits + newly-produced artifacts; everything else resolved by reference to the parent bundle. Smallest footprint; most plumbing; inspect needs overlay-aware resolution.

options.label: Hybrid: sibling bundle for new artifacts, overlay manifest pinning unchanged ones by hash
options.description: Replay dir contains only changed artifacts plus an overlay.json listing {path → originalSha256} for unchanged ones. Tamper-evident and compact.

questions.title: How does prune (OP-08) treat replay bundles?
questions.context: Phase 9 OP-08 prune has a lineage-preservation guarantee. Replays are a new lineage relationship.

options.label: Replays inherit parent’s prune protection
options.description: If parent run is protected, replays are protected. If parent is prunable, replays go too (unless individually protected by status).

options.label: Replays are independently prunable by their own status
options.description: Treat replay like any other run. Risk: pruning the parent orphans the replay’s overlay references (only matters if (c)/(d) chosen for Q-08).

options.label: Replays are never auto-pruned in v1
options.description: Conservative posture: counterfactual debug data is high-signal-per-byte. Revisit in v2.

questions.title: Replay command shape on factory-cli
questions.context: Phase 9 OP-07 pinned a multi-subcommand binary: run | status | resume | cancel | inspect | deliver | prune. Replay is a new verb.

options.label: New top-level verb: factory-cli replay --run <id> --edit <path>=<file>
options.description: First-class command. Mirrors inspect/deliver. Composable with --json-only contract.

options.label: Subverb under inspect: factory-cli inspect <id> replay --edit ...
options.description: Groups under "inspection" mental model. Stretches inspect’s scope (currently read-only).

options.label: Flag on run: factory-cli run --replay <id> --edit ...
options.description: Treats replay as a special run mode. Conceptually unifies; conflates fresh-run and replay UX.

questions.title: Edit input format on the CLI
questions.context: The operator needs to point at an edited artifact. JSON files are the obvious input shape.

options.label: --edit <path>=<file> (repeatable for multi if Q-01 picks b/c)
options.description: Each flag pairs a bundle-relative path with a local file. Familiar, scriptable.

options.label: --edit-set <manifest.json>
options.description: A single EditSet file lists all edits. Better for committed regression fixtures.

options.label: Both — --edit for ad-hoc, --edit-set for fixtures
options.description: Most flexible; small extra surface area.

questions.title: Delivery from replayed runs
questions.context: Success criterion: "Replay-with-edit defaults to no delivery — no PRs, no pushes; opt-in flag required and audited." Phase 12 stabilized the authority boundary; replay must compose cleanly.

options.label: Hard-disable: replay can never deliver, no flag
options.description: Replays are forensic-only. Simplest authority story. Loses the "verify the fix actually ships" use case.

options.label: Default off; --allow-delivery requires manifest opt-in + audit log entry
options.description: Per success criterion. Audit entry written to admission-decisions.jsonl with the editSet hash.

options.label: Default off; opt-in via factory-config.json replay.allowDelivery + per-invocation flag
options.description: Two-key for delivery from replay. Strictest path; matches Phase 2 two-key launch posture.

questions.title: Admission re-entry on replay
questions.context: Phase 1 admission gates apply to fresh runs. Editing the intent or planning-result of a replay would re-enter admission. Editing pile outputs is below the gate.

options.label: Replay re-runs admission gates against any edited admission-input artifact
options.description: If the edit touches intent/plan/capability inputs, gates re-fire. Matches "replay surfaces refusal evidence the same way a fresh run does".

options.label: Replay refuses edits to admission-input artifacts; only post-admission edits allowed
options.description: Editable allowlist (Q-02) excludes anything that would re-enter admission. Cleaner separation; loses "what if the plan had been different?" counterfactuals.

options.label: Replay allows the edit but always uses original run’s admission decisions
options.description: Runs from edit point with cached admission. Fastest; opens an authority hole if the edit changes capability requirements.

questions.title: Counterfactual signal feeding Phase 8 evolution
questions.context: Phase 8 (evaluation+evolution) is largely complete. Replay-with-edit could supply counterfactual data to evolve specs/plans ("this judge was the bottleneck"). Phase 13 success criterion: "Eval integration path is decided" — in-scope vs deferred is fine, just must be documented.

options.label: In-scope: replay produces an eval-counterfactual artifact consumed by Phase 8
options.description: Replay writes evaluation/counterfactual.json (delta vs parent run’s eval). Phase 8 evolution-runner reads it. More integration work; immediate signal payoff.

options.label: Deferred: design the artifact shape only, no Phase 8 wiring this phase
options.description: Replay emits the counterfactual artifact in a stable schema; Phase 8 hookup tracked as a follow-up requirement (e.g. EVAL-05). Lets Phase 13 ship without dragging Phase 8.

options.label: Out of scope entirely — replays are forensic-only in v1
options.description: No counterfactual artifact; eval integration revisited only when an explicit later phase asks. Smallest scope.
